lede: A look at January 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Marc Guiu and Ante Žižić, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Koszalin escape room fire
events.desc: A fire in an escape room in Koszalin, Poland, kills five teenagers through carbon monoxide poisoning.

events.title: Hennenman–Kroonstad train crash
events.desc: Hennenman–Kroonstad train crash: A passenger train operated by Shosholoza Meyl collides with a truck on a level crossing at Geneva Station between Hennenman and Kroonstad, Free State, South Africa. Twenty people are killed and 260 injured.

events.title: Burj Khalifa
events.desc: The Burj Khalifa, the current tallest building in the world, officially opens in Dubai.

events.title: Spirit (rover)
events.desc: Spirit, a NASA Mars rover, lands successfully on Mars at 04:35 UTC.

events.title: TOPS-10
events.desc: This date overflowed the 12-bit field that had been used in TOPS-10. There were numerous problems and crashes related to this bug while an alternative format was developed.

events.title: Rose Heilbron
events.desc: Rose Heilbron becomes the first female judge to sit at the Old Bailey in London, UK.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: Chinese and North Korean forces capture Seoul for the second time.

events.title: Aeneas Mackintosh
events.desc: Explorer Aeneas Mackintosh of the Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition escaped death by fleeing across ice floes.

events.title: Sino-French War
events.desc: Sino-French War: French troops under General Oscar de Négrier defeat a numerically superior Qing force at Núi Bop in northern Vietnam.

events.title: Solomon Northup
events.desc: After having been kidnapped and sold into slavery in the American South, Solomon Northup regains his freedom; his memoir Twelve Years a Slave later becomes a national bestseller.

births.name: Richard R. Schrock
births.line: American chemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Humphrey Carpenter, English radio host and author
Phil Lynott, Irish singer-songwriter, bass player, and producer
Nellie Cashman, American nurse, restaurateur, entrepreneur, and gold prospector

observances.text: The eleventh of the Twelve Days of Christmas. (Western Christianity)
